title: Elatostema acutidentatum
intro: Elatostema acutidentatum, also known as Sharp-toothed Elatostema, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema acutidentatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elatostema acutidentatum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a topical paste or cream to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Elatostema acutidentatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This family consists of plants commonly known as nettle or figworts. The genus Elatostema, to which Elatostema acutidentatum belongs, comprises over 300 species of small, herbaceous plants. The classification of Elatostema acutidentatum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. It is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its unique, heart-shaped leaves.
